title: dichanthelium ovale subsp. villosissimum
intro: Dichanthelium ovale subsp. villosissimum, also known as the hairy panic grass, is a subspecies of grass belonging to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and potentially lowering blood sugar levels. Hairy panic grass can be prepared as a tea or infused oil, and it is also used in herbal remedies and supplements.
